summary: Added terminalScheduleBlock helper + guards in set_task_status and update_task; mirrors HTTP guard in UpdateTaskStatus.js:147-160. All 13 tests pass.

findings.claim: MCP tools bypass HTTP terminal-requires-schedule guard — raw DB constraint fires instead of clean isError
findings.evidence: Added module-level TERMINAL_REQUIRES_SCHEDULE=['done','skip','cancel'] and async terminalScheduleBlock(existing,status,willBeScheduled,userId) above registerTaskTools; returns rejection message string or null (rolling-exempt via isRollingMaster lookup on master_id||source_id)

findings.claim: set_task_status: no guard before DB write for terminal status on unscheduled task
findings.evidence: Inserted `var _termBlock = await terminalScheduleBlock(existing, status, !!existing.scheduled_at, userId); if (_termBlock) { return { content:[{type:'text',text:_termBlock}], isError:true }; }` before `var update = { status: ... }`

findings.claim: update_task: no guard before DB write for terminal status on unscheduled task
findings.evidence: Inserted _willBeScheduled computation (covers existing.scheduled_at OR fields.scheduledAt OR fields.date) and terminalScheduleBlock call before the isLocked branch, so both locked and unlocked write paths are blocked
